name: United Kingdom

visas.name: Skilled Worker Visa
visas.description: Main work visa for people with a job offer from a UK employer who holds a sponsor licence. Covers most professional and technical roles. Replaced the old Tier 2 visa.
requirements: Job offer from a UK Home Office-approved sponsor
Role must be on the eligible occupations list
Salary must meet the minimum threshold (£38,700/year as of 2024, or going rate for the job)
English language at B1 level (CEFR)
visas.leadsTo: Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) after 5 years

visas.name: Global Talent Visa
visas.description: For internationally recognised leaders and emerging talent in academia, research, digital technology, arts, culture, or film. No job offer required.
requirements: Endorsement from an approved UK endorsing body
Evidence of exceptional talent or exceptional promise in your field
No minimum salary requirement
visas.leadsTo: ILR after 3 years (exceptional talent) or 5 years (exceptional promise)

visas.name: Graduate Visa
visas.description: For international students who have completed a UK degree. Work or look for work after graduation — no job offer required.
requirements: Completed a UK bachelor's, master's, or PhD degree on a Student visa
Apply from within the UK before your Student visa expires
visas.leadsTo: Can switch to Skilled Worker visa once you have a qualifying job

visas.name: Family Visa (Spouse / Partner / Child)
visas.description: Join a British citizen or settled person in the UK. Covers spouses, civil partners, unmarried partners, and children under 18.
requirements: Sponsor is a British citizen or has ILR / Settled Status
Sponsor meets minimum income requirement (£29,000/year as of 2024)
Genuine relationship
English language at A1 level (for spouse/partner)

visas.name: Student Visa
visas.description: Study at a UK university, college, or school sponsored by a licensed student sponsor. Most students can work up to 20 hours/week during term.
requirements: Unconditional offer from a UK licensed student sponsor
Sufficient funds (£1,334/month in London or £1,023/month elsewhere for up to 9 months)
English language ability (typically B2 CEFR for degree-level study)
Parental consent if under 18
visas.leadsTo: Graduate visa after completing studies; can switch to Skilled Worker with a job offer

visas.name: Innovator Founder Visa
visas.description: For experienced businesspeople who want to set up or run an innovative, viable, and scalable business in the UK. Must be endorsed by an approved body.
requirements: Endorsement from an approved UK endorsing body
Innovative, viable, and scalable business idea (or existing business)
At least £50,000 investment funding (may be waived by the endorser)

citizenship.name: Naturalisation
citizenship.requiresResidency: 5 years (3 years if married to a British citizen)

benefits.childBenefit: Child Benefit — £25.60/week for eldest child, £16.95/week for each additional child (2024/25)
benefits.healthInsurance: NHS — free at point of use for visa holders who paid the Immigration Health Surcharge
benefits.unemployment: Universal Credit — replaces most benefits; amount depends on circumstances

taxNotes: UK taxes worldwide income for tax residents. Non-domiciled residents may claim remittance basis in early years. Self-assessment required for self-employed and high earners.
